{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = "adamw"","old":"OPTIMIZER = "adam"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"build_scheduler","new":"","old":"","op":"replace_function","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    pct_start = min(0.3, 32 / max(total_steps, 1))
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=total_steps,
        pct_start=pct_start,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=20.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Under a fixed 256-step budget, a short-horizon schedule should matter more than long-run settings. This candidate increases sample coverage with a larger batch, switches to AdamW, and uses a OneCycle schedule tuned to the exact step budget to front-load progress while remaining simple and contract-safe.","secondary_modes":["indexing","micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The short-budget optimization changes already improved early learning. {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"                layers.append(nn.LayerNorm(hidden_dim))","old":"                layers.append(nn.BatchNorm1d(hidden_dim))","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","old":"    criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.1)","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"With only 256 training steps, BatchNorm running statistics can be noisy in a flat MLP and hurt evaluation.
